name: Apple Cheddar Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Enriched Corn Meal (Aunt Jemima or Quaker)
8 cups Peeled, sliced apples (about 8 medium apples)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, corn meal, and salt in a large bowl.
2. Cut in the margarine and shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Stir in the cheese.
4. Add water, 1 tablespoon at a time, stirring lightly until the dough forms a ball.
5. Divide the dough into two parts; shape each into a ball.
6. Wrap securely in plastic wrap or wax paper; chill for about 30 minutes.
7. Roll out one ball of dough on a lightly floured surface to form an 11-inch circle and fit it loosely into a 9-inch pie plate; trim.
8. Roll the remaining dough to form a 12-inch square. Cut into 12 x 3/4-inch strips.
9. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining flour, apples, sugar, and cinnamon; spoon into the crust.
10. Dot with the remaining 2 tablespoons margarine.
11. Weave the strips over the filling to make a lattice top.
12. Trim the edges even with the rim of the pie plate.
13. Fold the lower crust over the strips and seal.
14. Flute the edges; cut slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape.
15. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
16. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until the crust is light golden brown, shielding the edges with aluminum foil if necessary.
17. Cool slightly before serving.
Note: To make a double-crust pie, roll the remaining dough to form a 10-inch circle instead of cutting strips. Prepare the filling as directed, place the top crust over it, trim, and bake.
